bull.b: SelectQuote has moved from losses to sustained positive earnings and meaningful revenue growth, reflecting improved scale. Over the next 2–6 months this reduces execution risk, helps cover fixed costs, and supports reinvestment in marketing and operations to entrench growth trends.

The Senior (Medicare) franchise consistently delivers high EBITDA margins (~25%+) and demonstrated multi-year seasonally strong profitability. Coupled with a large commissions backbook, this provides durable renewal cash flows and lowers revenue volatility versus single‑period sales.

bull.t: SelectRx Scale, Efficiency And Membership Growth
bull.b: Healthcare Services (SelectRx) shows structural scale: strong membership growth, steep prescription volume gains, an efficiency-led path to a $40–50M EBITDA run‑rate, and facility capacity upside. These operational improvements support durable margin expansion and cash generation as volume scales.

bear.b: Despite accounting profits, recent negative operating and free cash flow indicate earnings are not reliably converting to cash. Over months this raises funding and execution risk, constrains deleveraging and reinvestment, and makes the business sensitive to working capital and timing swings.

bear.t: Reliance On Commissions Receivable & One-time Gains
bear.b: Results were bolstered by a non‑recurring $14M commission estimate adjustment and a nearly $1B commissions receivable balance. Dependence on large receivables and accounting adjustments raises recognition and timing risk, potentially inflating short‑term profitability and obscuring true cash conversion.

bear.t: Residual Leverage And Historic Capital Stress
bear.b: The balance sheet has strengthened, yet the company recently carried high leverage and negative returns. If cash flow slips or MA approval cadence weakens, the capital structure could be re‑tested, limiting strategic flexibility and raising refinancing or cost-of-capital risks over the medium term.
